feed.title: Ayush Shukla
feed.subtitle: I like building things that actually work and writing about how they work under the hood. My focus is systems-level thinking, algorithms, and the math that makes everything click. This site is where I drop writeups, experiments, and technical notes—sometimes neat, sometimes messy, always honest.

entry.title: MathJax Demo
entry.content:
This post demonstrates how to use MathJax with Jekyll Minima.

Inline math

You can write inline math like this: $E = mc^2$ or equivalently (\frac{a}{b}).

Display math

And block equations like this:

\[\int_{0}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} \, dx = \frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{2}\]

Or using \[ ... \] syntax:

\[\nabla \cdot \vec{E} = \frac{\rho}{\epsilon_0}\]
entry.summary: This post demonstrates how to use MathJax with Jekyll Minima.

entry.title: AsciiDoc Demo
entry.content:
AsciiDoc Demo

This post is written in AsciiDoc! 🎉

Inline math

You can write inline math like this: \(E = mc^2\).

Block math

And block equations like this:

\[\int_0^\infty e^{-x^2} dx = \frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{2}\]

This is a note block.
